title: Pure Simulation Core, Effect Adapters, Events, and Dependency Direction · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs pure simulation core, effect adapters, events, and dependency direction from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for testable game core and adapter architecture after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.
Keep display, events, devices, audio, assets, persistence, clock, logging, and packaging behind thin owned adapters.
Emit immutable collision, score, life, wave, cue, and transition records and consume them in explicit order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Pure Simulation · changed case · Review Pure Simulation
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 7487 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Pure Simulation without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Pure Simulation · changed case · Review Pure Simulation.

lines.text: Headless import success proves the complete desktop game works.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove the pure core runs without pygame and the adapter composition runs headlessly without claiming visual or device quality. Artifact under review: testable game core and adapter architecture.

options.text: The Architecture Evidence record says testable game core and adapter architecture looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Architecture Evidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Architecture Evidence for testable game core and adapter architecture.
The record names the assumption, applies Architecture Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Architecture Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Architecture Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 7537 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove the pure core runs without pygame and the adapter composition runs headlessly without claiming visual or device quality.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Architecture Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Architecture Evidence through the concrete testable game core and adapter architecture case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Architecture Evidence, keep Architecture Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove the pure core runs without pygame and the adapter composition runs headlessly without claiming visual or device quality.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Architecture Evidence, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Architecture Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Architecture Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A review Asteroids team handles case 7537 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove the pure core runs without pygame and the adapter composition runs headlessly without claiming visual or device quality.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Architecture Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Headless import success proves the complete desktop game works.
